description: Pharmacy refill escalation meeting. Should produce stories around medication-refill push notifications and household-account verification. Expect a HIPAA conflict (surfacing medication names in notifications) and a duplicate against NS-176 (Pharmacy refill SMS reminder).

deterministic_metrics.name: acceptance_criteria_well_formed
observations: 4/4 stories have AC count in range

deterministic_metrics.name: expected_duplicates_found
observations: Expected duplicate (['sms', 'refill', 'reminder'] ↔ NS-176) not found at confidence ≥ medium

reasons.ac_quality: Most criteria are concrete and testable with clear Given/When/Then structure, though a few (like ST-04's pointer to NS-176) are meta-level rather than genuinely testable acceptance criteria.
reasons.priority_justification: Priority rationales cite specific business impacts (revenue loss, HIPAA violations, customer demographics) with evidence from speakers, though ST-04's 'Medium' could better explain why it's not High given the accessibility gap.
reasons.story_granularity: Stories are well-sized single deliverables (proactive push, opt-in capability, patient routing, SMS prioritization), though ST-04 is unusual as a pointer story rather than implementation work.
reasons.tag_accuracy: Tags are consistently applied and appropriate (pharmacy, mobile-app, compliance, security), with only one non-canonical tag ('sms') flagged by guardrails, showing good vocabulary discipline.
reasons.conflict_reasoning: ST-01's conflict with C-09 is incorrectly reasoned—the story explicitly routes through NSID per C-04 and doesn't describe account-level delivery; the C-10 conflict is valid but marked 'medium' when missing mandatory audit logging should be 'high'.

llm_judge.overall_comment: This is a strong backlog synthesis with well-structured stories, clear compliance awareness, and good traceability to source topics. The conflict detection has significant errors, incorrectly flagging ST-01 for C-09 when the story text explicitly addresses patient-scoped routing, and the gaps section identifies real infrastructure needs that should have been flagged as conflicts with specific stories.
